subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: The auth:login command lets you log in to Contentstack and save the session

options.description: Email address of your Contentstack account
Password can be passed in command

subcommands.name: auth:tokens:add
subcommands.description: Lets you add an existing management token from your Contentstack account and save it to the session for further use

options.description: Alias (name) you want to assign to the token
Alias (name) you want to assign to the token
Flag to set delivery token
API key of the stack where the token exists
Value of the token
Force token replace

subcommands.name: auth:tokens:remove
subcommands.description: The auth:tokens:remove command lets you delete a management/ delivery token from your local CLI session

options.description: Alias (name) you want to assign to the token
Ignores if token not present. Command

subcommands.name: cm:bootstrap
subcommands.description: The 'Bootstrap' plugin in Contentstack CLI allows users to automate the process of project setup for starter apps

options.description: The name of the starter app you want to use. Example: reactjs-starter, nextjs-starter, gatsby-starter, angular-starter, nuxt-starter
Directory/path to setup the project. If directory name has a space then provide the path as a string or escap the space using back slash as shown in the below example
Sample or Starter app
Provide stack API key to seed content
Provide organization UID to create a new stack
Name of the new stack that will be created

subcommands.name: auth:tokens
subcommands.description: The auth:tokens command lists the existing tokens added to the session

options.description: To show extra columns
To display specific columns, separated by comma
Output is csv format
To filter the property by partial string matching, ex: name=foo
To hide table’s header in output
To not truncate output to fit screen
To display output in a particular format
To sort the list, prepend '-' to sort in a descending order

subcommands.name: config:get:region
subcommands.description: The config:get:region command will return the name of the region on which you are performing actions in Contentstack, via the CLI

subcommands.name: config:set:region
subcommands.description: The config:set:region command lets you select a region, from the available Contentstack regions, to perform actions using the CLI

args.name: region
args.description: The ContentStack AWS region you want to use

subcommands.name: cm:stacks:export
subcommands.description: The cm:stacks:export command lets you export content from one stack to another

options.description: [optional] path of the config
API key of the source stack
The management token of the source stack from which you will export content
The path or the location in your file system to store the exported content. For e.g., ./content
The name of the branch where you want to export your content. If you don’t mention the branch name, then by default the content will be exported from all the branches of your stack

[optional] Specific module name. If not specified, the export command will export all the modules to the stack. The available modules are assets, content-types, entries, environments, extensions, glob
[optional] content type
[optional] use when assets are secured

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: The auth:logout command lets you log out of Contentstack and clear the session

subcommands.name: whoami
subcommands.description: The csdx auth:whoami command returns the username (email address) of the user who is currently logged in to the session

options.description: Show help for csdx
